question: [File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with realm.num format strings (e.g., 0.12345678901) that pass EntityId.isValidEntityId() and EntityId.parseString(), causing getEncodedId() to return getEncodedId() without a DB query but then downstream isValidAccount() executes entityExistenceQuery for each, sustaining a DB query storm that degrades 30%+ of mirror node processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of request deduplication in getEncodedId() by sending many concurrent requests with the same EVM address, each independently executing entityFromEvmAddressQuery (since EVM address lookups are not cached at the service layer), multiplying DB load and exhausting connection pools on 30%+ of mirror node instances?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getEntityIdFromEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with EVM addresses that are valid hex strings but contain only uppercase letters (e.g., AAAA...AAAA), causing getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() to execute entityFromEvmAddressQuery with Buffer.from() converting them to binary, generating sustained DB load that exhausts connection pools on 30%+ of mirror node instances?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that AccountAlias.fromString() calls base32.decode() with loose=true option (allowing auto-correction of 0->O, 1->L, 8->B), causing getEncodedId() to process more alias variants than expected and execute more entityFromAliasQuery DB calls, amplifying DB load and degrading 30%+ of mirror node processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with negative encoded entity ID strings (e.g., -9223372036854775808 matching /^-?\\d{1,19}$/) that pass EntityId.isValidEntityId() and EntityId.parseString(), causing getEncodedId() to return a negative BigInt that is then passed to isValidAccount() executing entityExistenceQuery with a negative ID, generating DB errors that crash the handler?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountFromAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with alias strings containing the maximum allowed digits prefix (e.g., 99999.99999.AAAA) that pass AccountAlias.isValid(), causing AccountAlias.fromString() to call AccountAlias.validate() which throws InvalidArgumentError for unsupported shard/realm, and by flooding with such requests, cause error handling overhead that degrades 30%+ of mirror node processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential nature of getEncodedId() (first checks EntityId.isValidEntityId, then AccountAlias.isValid) by crafting strings that fail both checks but only after expensive regex evaluation, causing CPU overhead per request that degrades 30%+ of mirror node processing capacity when sustained at high request rates?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getEntityIdFromEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with EVM addresses in shard.realm.evmAddress format (e.g., 0.0.ABCD...1234) where the EVM address portion is a valid opaque address (non-zero prefix), causing getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() to execute entityFromEvmAddressQuery for each request, sustaining a DB query storm that degrades 30%+ of mirror node processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getEncodedId() makes no distinction between cached and uncached entity ID lookups for EVM addresses (the LRU cache in entityId.js only caches parsed EntityId objects, not DB lookup results), causing every unique EVM address request to hit the DB, sustaining a query storm that degrades 30%+ of mirror node processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountFromAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with alias strings that decode to the same binary value via base32 loose decoding (e.g., 0->O substitution), causing entityFromAliasQuery to repeatedly query for the same alias value, generating sustained DB load that exhausts connection pools on 30%+ of mirror node instances?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/tokens endpoint calls both getEncodedId() and isValidAccount() sequentially, each executing a separate DB query, by flooding this endpoint with unique alias strings to create a 2x DB query amplification that exhausts connection pools on 30%+ of mirror node instances?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards endpoint calls both getEncodedId() and isValidAccount() sequentially, each executing a separate DB query, by flooding this endpoint with unique EVM addresses to create a 2x DB query amplification that exhausts connection pools on 30%+ of mirror node instances?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getEntityIdFromEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with EVM addresses that have a non-zero prefix (making them opaque EVM addresses, not account num aliases), causing getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() to always execute entityFromEvmAddressQuery (since these are not cached by the EntityId LRU cache as resolved IDs), sustaining a DB query storm that degrades 30%+ of mirror node processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getEncodedId() is exported as a singleton (export default new EntityService()) shared across all requests, meaning there is no per-request isolation, and by flooding with concurrent requests, cause shared state corruption or resource exhaustion that degrades 30%+ of mirror node processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with strings that are exactly at the boundary of EntityId.isValidEntityId() acceptance (e.g., 9999.99999.274877906943 matching /^(\\d{1,4}\\.)?\\d{1,5}\\.\\d{1,12}$/), causing getEncodedId() to parse them and return encoded IDs without DB queries, but then downstream isValidAccount() executes entityExistenceQuery for each, sustaining a DB query storm that degrades 30%+ of mirror node processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getEntityIdFromEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the inconsistency between entityFromAliasQuery (uses coalesce(deleted, false) <> true) and entityFromEvmAddressQuery (uses deleted <> true without coalesce), where entities with NULL deleted field are excluded from EVM address lookups but included in alias lookups, causing unexpected NotFoundError responses that trigger error handling overhead and degrade 30%+ of mirror node processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getEncodedId() has no result caching at the service layer (unlike the EntityId LRU cache which only caches parsed EntityId objects), causing every alias or EVM address lookup to hit the DB regardless of how many times the same value is requested, sustaining a DB query storm that degrades 30%+ of mirror node processing capacity?
